Problem_Solved: Instantly filters users into Startup (0% interest) vs Revenue-Based paths.
Founders need a clean, investor-ready place to host the deck, financials, and team info without emailing messy attachments.
Target_Persona: Founders raising capital; acquisition entrepreneurs; indie SaaS builders
Inputs: Company basics; pitch deck; financials; team bio; metrics
Outputs: Password-protected data room link + upload structure
Why_It_Matters: Every investor touchpoint is a trust test. This makes you look organized in minutes.
Example_Use_Case: You’re pitching 7 angels this week. You send one clean link instead of a Dropbox mess.

Problem_Solved: People guess at fundability. This gives an instant ‘where you stand’ snapshot and next steps.
Target_Persona: SMB owners; ecommerce sellers; gig/self-employed; agencies screening clients
Inputs: Monthly revenue; time in business; industry; credit band (optional)
Outputs: Fundability score band + best-fit product paths
Why_It_Matters: Turns vague interest into a concrete number—and a clear next action.
Example_Use_Case: You’re doing $35k/mo. The widget tells you what’s realistic today and what to fix next.

Problem_Solved: Calculates the exact MCA amount needed to cover payroll while waiting for a Net-30 check.
Quantifies revenue lost per hour during an inventory stockout vs cost of capital.
Most offers hide the real cost. This makes payback and trade-offs obvious (MCA vs LOC vs equity).
Target_Persona: Founders comparing offers; brokers; finance ops leads
Inputs: Offer amount; term; rate/factor; fees; expected revenue
Why_It_Matters: Transparency as a weapon—prevents expensive ‘fast money’ mistakes.
Example_Use_Case: You got a factor-rate offer. This shows the true APR and cheaper alternatives.

Problem_Solved: Visualizes how dilution compounds across rounds vs non-dilutive paths.
Target_Persona: Startups & SaaS founders considering VC vs non-dilutive capital
Inputs: Valuation; raise amount; assumed future rounds
Why_It_Matters: People don’t ‘feel’ dilution until they see it. This makes the pain visible.
Example_Use_Case: You think $500k is ‘small.’ The simulator shows what it costs by Series B.

Problem_Solved: Extracts terms from offers and calculates true APR + red flags (factor-rate traps).
Target_Persona: SMBs offered MCA/fast funding; founders under time pressure
Inputs: Offer PDF or terms; amount; factor/rate; holdback; term
Outputs: True cost report + red-flag list + safer alternatives
Why_It_Matters: Saves you from signing the financial equivalent of a bear trap.
Example_Use_Case: You got ‘easy approval’ funding. This reveals the real APR before you sign.

Problem_Solved: Projects burn + ‘death date’ and shows how different capital injections extend runway.
Target_Persona: Startups; agencies; ecommerce brands watching cash
Inputs: Cash on hand; monthly burn; revenue trend (or connect Stripe/Plaid)
Why_It_Matters: Urgency becomes math. Math becomes action.
Example_Use_Case: You have 63 days left. This shows what $50k buys you in time.

Problem_Solved: Turns messy founder answers into a lender/investor-ready executive summary / deal memo.
Target_Persona: Founders applying for capital; acquisition buyers; brokers prepping submissions
Inputs: 5–6 business questions; basic financials; use of funds
Outputs: Banker-style deal memo PDF + submission-ready summary
Why_It_Matters: Professional packaging increases approvals and speed.
Example_Use_Case: You’re applying to 3 lenders. One memo makes you look 10x more organized.

Problem_Solved: Simulates downturn scenarios to reveal cashflow vulnerabilities and buffers (LOC/working capital).
Target_Persona: SMBs; ecommerce; agencies; local service businesses
Inputs: Business URL; model description; revenue + expense ranges
Outputs: Risk report + mitigation actions + capital buffer plan
Why_It_Matters: Better to discover weakness in a dashboard than in bankruptcy court.
Example_Use_Case: A 20% revenue drop—this shows where you snap and how to reinforce.

Problem_Solved: Organizes, verifies, and packages business docs into a funding-ready bundle.
Target_Persona: Founders who hate paperwork; SMB owners; brokers collecting docs
Inputs: EIN letter; Articles/Operating Agreement; ID; voided check; bank statements
Outputs: Verified doc set + standardized naming + ZIP export
Why_It_Matters: Cuts underwriting friction and prevents ‘missing-doc’ delays.
Example_Use_Case: You upload a mess. You download a clean, lender-ready package.

Problem_Solved: Creates a mobile-first ‘pitch page’ so founders can share one clean link (not a PDF).
Target_Persona: Founders raising capital on LinkedIn/Twitter; creators; indie SaaS
Outputs: Scrollable pitch page link + gated downloads + booking button
Why_It_Matters: Modern fundraising happens on phones. PDFs die there.
Example_Use_Case: You DM an investor. You send a pitch link that actually converts.

Problem_Solved: Lets users toggle funding options and see runway/cashflow impact like a finance sandbox.
Target_Persona: Founders deciding between LOC/RBF/advance; finance ops leads
Outputs: Runway + cashflow chart with option toggles
Why_It_Matters: Turns ‘maybe’ into numbers—and numbers into decisions.
Example_Use_Case: You toggle $50k LOC vs $20k advance and instantly see the tradeoffs.

Problem_Solved: A persistent dashboard that surfaces cash signals + daily ‘wake-up call’ insights.
Target_Persona: Operators; SMB owners; agencies; ecommerce founders
Outputs: Daily pulse insights + cash alerts + action list
Why_It_Matters: Creates a habit. When you need funding, the button is already there.
Example_Use_Case: It flags dead subscriptions and cash leaks before they become emergencies.

Problem_Solved: Gives ruthless, specific feedback on decks and outputs a ‘roast report’ + fundability score.
Target_Persona: Founders; acquisition entrepreneurs; anyone pitching investors/lenders
Why_It_Matters: A good deck gets meetings. A bad deck gets ignored.
Example_Use_Case: Upload deck → get a punchy list of fixes before you send it to anyone important.
